title: Cyperella campestris
intro: Cyperella campestris, also known as Field Cyperus, is a type of perennial plant belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as digestive issues and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Cyperella campestris include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cyperella campestris is often used in the form of teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as in traditional Chinese medicine formulas to support digestive health and immune function.
taxonomy: Cyperella campestris belongs to the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. It is a genus of plants within the order Poales. The classification of Cyperella campestris also includes it being a member of the kingdom Plantae and the domain Eukaryota. It is a type of sedge, which is often found in wetland areas and has adapted to grow in a variety of conditions. Cyperella campestris is known to be a relatively rare species, adding to its unique classification within the plant kingdom.

preparations: Cyperella campestris is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in folk herbalism. Herbal preparations of Cyperella campestris typically involve infusing the plant's leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea.
